msgs.text:
Turn 72 — Certificate D nonlinear verifier addendum I used only the three files attached to this message. I did not open or rely on the un-attached Turn 70/71 artifacts; I reconstructed the next appendix piece from the attached history and problem statement.The Turn 71 state says the proof has already been frozen as Certificates 0–F, with the conclusion that an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH descends to ΓS≅GΓH. It also says the remaining external reproducibility item is to attach nonlinear verifier material, especially around Certificate D and the D.3 stack-rank 60→61.

I produced two Turn 72 artifacts:

Download the Certificate D nonlinear verifier addendum Download the D.3 nonlinear verifier contract

What Turn 72 adds The new addendum inserts a dedicated nonlinear-verifier section under Certificate D. It does not reopen degree 16, the A2 -layer, the t-layer, or any new coefficient-grade case. It records the reproducible verifier contract for the nonlinear blocks D.1–D.4, with D.3 expanded most fully.The central D.3 contract is now written as a deterministic matrix construction:D5,6,7:3L5⊕3L6⊕3L7⟶T6⊕T7⊕T8,built by postmultiplying one of Y0,Y1,q by actual restricted representatives in degrees 5,6,7, expandingS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q,and retaining homogeneous degrees 6,7,8. The expected residual checks aredimL1,…,dimL8=[2,3,2,6,6,11,18,36],S(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))X−2 ≡1(modI9),and rankD5,6,7=60.

The coefficient row μ4B4 is specified separately overA2=R/(4,t).

The addendum states the degree-four lower ghost mapβ4(u0,u1,uq)=[B,u0]+[A,u1]+[B,uq],the detectorμ4=coeffe1[2]+coeffe3[2],and the transfer functional ℓ:T5→F2 satisfying ℓ∘β4=μ4.The verifier then computes the actual Z/4 coefficient-2 degree-5 ghost for each of the 105 continuation columns, applies ℓ, and obtains the row μ4B4. The decisive certificate remains the rank jump rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7μ4B4)=61.This is exactly the executable D.3 certificate recorded in the sweep: since H4(2)is one-dimensional, the rank jump proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed μ4=1 scalar to 0.

The addendum also records the surrounding nonlinear ledger:D.1: ker∂8↠L5[2],D.2: the residual degree-16 lower-base λ-toggle,D.3: the A2, d=4, μ4 -toggle via stack-rank 60→61,D.4: θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].This matches the frozen certificate ledger in the attached history, where the nonlinear blocks D.1–D.4 are the finite exceptional pieces before the residual and coefficient inductions take over.

Status

Turn 72 is a presentation/reproducibility step. It packages the nonlinear Certificate D verifier contract in manuscript-ready form and adds a compact Markdown contract for the D.3 stack-rank check.It does not claim that a full nonlinear executable script was regenerated in this turn. The addendum is the precise appendix contract that such a script or pivot-table appendix should satisfy.

ESTABLISHED: A Certificate D nonlinear verifier addendum has been produced. It gives a reproducible D.3 matrix-and-row construction, states the expected residual and A2 -coefficient checks, and records the decisive stack-rank 60→61. It also places D.1, D.2, and D.4 around D.3 as the complete nonlinear certificate ledger.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The remaining external reproducibility item is still to attach a fully executable nonlinear verifier script or explicit pivot tables realizing the D.1–D.4 checks, especially the D.3 matrix construction and stack-rank computation.

NEXT: Turn the D.3 contract into an executable nonlinear verifier script, or attach pivot tables for D5,6,7, μ4B4, and the stacked 61-rank row reduction, without reopening any new obstruction layer.[TURN72-CERT-D-NONLINEAR-VERIFIER-ADDENDUM]
